name: Linear Programming (Simplex / Interior-Point)
applicable_scenarios: optimize a linear objective subject to linear equality and inequality constraints
resource allocation, production planning, diet problems, transportation
large sparse LPs with thousands of variables and constraints
math_form: \min_x c^T x \text{ s.t. } A_{ub} x \le b_{ub}, \; A_{eq} x = b_{eq}, \; l \le x \le u
python_template:
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import linprog

def solve_lp(c, A_ub=None, b_ub=None, A_eq=None, b_eq=None, bounds=None):
    res = linprog(
        c=c, A_ub=A_ub, b_ub=b_ub, A_eq=A_eq, b_eq=b_eq,
        bounds=bounds, method='highs',
    )
    return res
typical_cases: factory production mix to maximize profit
shipping goods from warehouses to customers at min cost
common_pitfalls: unbounded or infeasible problems need careful status checks
mixing integer variables requires MILP (not LP); use PuLP / pyomo
numerical scaling matters; pre-normalize coefficients for HiGHS
the simplex method's worst case is exponential, though rare in practice
